//: CAWS Command-Safety Adapter Sidecar — EXAMPLE. Copy to .caws/command-adapters.json to extend the command-safety classifier with adapter rows for tools the shipped defaults (kubectl/docker/terraform/aws/curl/kill/shred) don't cover. Validated against .caws/command-adapters.schema.json. See docs/architecture/command-capability-taxonomy.md. JSON (not YAML) because the hook parses it with the python stdlib `json` module only — PyYAML is not available in the hook runtime.
//authority: A row maps tool + subcommand-path (+ resource aliases) -> ABSTRACT FACETS only. You CANNOT set a decision (allow/ask/deny), override the lattice, or invent a new kind/facet value. The lattice decides; you only describe WHAT a command does. A malformed or authority-exceeding sidecar is IGNORED and the hook surfaces a diagnostic `ask` — a bad config never weakens the gate.
